heading: Alternative fuels bus program
label: 6374b Alternative fuels bus program
notes: Amendments1992—Subsec. (a). substituted “alternative fuels” for “alcohol and buses capable of operating on natural gas” and “each of the various types of alternative fuel buses” for “both buses capable of operating on alcohol and buses capable of operating on natural gas”.Pub. L. 102–486
amendments: Amendments1992—Subsec. (a). substituted “alternative fuels” for “alcohol and buses capable of operating on natural gas” and “each of the various types of alternative fuel buses” for “both buses capable of operating on alcohol and buses capable of operating on natural gas”.Pub. L. 102–486

children.heading: Testing
children.label: a Testing
children.text: The Secretary, in cooperation with the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency and the Administrator of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, shall, beginning in the fiscal year ending , assist State and local government agencies in the testing in urban settings of buses capable of operating on alternative fuels for the emissions levels, durability, safety, and fuel economy of such buses, comparing the different types with each other and with diesel powered buses, as such buses will be required to operate under Federal safety and environmental standards applicable to such buses for the model year 1991. To the extent practicable, testing assisted under this section shall apply to each of the various types of alternative fuel buses.September 30, 1990

children.heading: Funding
children.label: b Funding
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for the period encompassing the fiscal years ending , , and , a total of $2,000,000 to carry out the purposes of this section.September 30, 1990September 30, 1991September 30, 1992

children.heading: “Bus” defined
children.label: c “Bus” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “bus” means a vehicle which is designed to transport 30 individuals or more.
